name: byte_indexing_with_la_fold_undo
title: Byte-array indexing + reverse GCC's la-offset folding
summary: Function loops over bytes inside a per-element struct, e.g. `BASE[i].field` where BASE is `extern s32`. m2c's pointer arithmetic doubles the multiplier (same as byte_indexing_with_drain_delay). Additionally, GCC pre-folds the constant byte offset into the address-load (`la $R, BASE+ofs`) where target keeps it on the lbu/sb. Three regfix rules undo the fold so the offsets re-appear on the load/store.
proof: func_800550E8 (commit 4d9be09) -- 8-iter loop clearing a single byte at offset 0x415, with arg0 selecting the 1100-byte struct.

structural: after byte-arithmetic C fix, our pipeline emits `la $R, D_<sym>+<offset>` (with offset folded in) and `lbu/sb $X, 0($R)`

rule_skeleton.instruction: Replace `(v * N) + &D_<sym>` with `(u8 *)&D_<sym> + v * N` to force byte arithmetic.

rule_skeleton.comment: drop the +<offset> from the la pseudo-insn
restore the offset on the load
restore the offset on the store

notes: Get LA_IDX, L_IDX, S_IDX from `dc.sh dump-text <func>` after the C edit + integration.
Same pattern applies to lh / lhu / lw / lb / sh / sw -- substitute the right load/store opcode.
Only LBU/SB cases handled in near_miss_attempt v1 detection; other widths require manual application via add-regfix.
